facts.text: Bal Harbour was born from a 1957 vision when developer Stanley Whitman bought 16 acres to build a luxury open‑air shopping mall.
The village sits on the northern tip of the barrier island that makes up Miami Beach, a fact that ties it directly to the city’s name.
The 1927 Army Corps of Engineers breakwater, later rebuilt as the Bal Harbour Jetty, was designed after extensive community input and competitive reviews.
In 1953, Arthur Godfrey became the first national television host to broadcast from Bal Harbour, reaching an audience of 40 to 60 million from the Kenilworth Hotel.
The original 645‑room Sheraton Bal Harbour hotel, once a celebrity haunt, was demolished in 2007 to make way for new development.
